name: Sam Adeyemi
publicRole: Junior Associate, joins in Year 4. The player's second direct report. MBA from a well-regarded programme, two years at a Big Four firm, clearly ambitious. Was explicitly recruited for the VP track.
privateGoal: Sam knows exactly what he wants and has a plan for getting it. The plan involves finding the right manager, learning what he can, and moving on before he outgrows the relationship. He is not cynical about this — it is how he believes careers work. He is also genuinely interested in the work and will invest fully as long as the investment makes sense to him.
contradiction: Presents as ambitious and open about it — which reads as refreshing honesty. The openness is real but the transparency is selective. He has already mapped three exit paths from his current role, and he decides once a quarter whether to activate them. He would describe this as 'keeping options open.' It is that. It is also a specific orientation toward people that they will eventually feel.

voicePattern.description: Polished, direct, good at adjusting register to match the room. Slightly too smooth in early stages — the smoothness is a trained behaviour from business school that he is gradually replacing with actual style. Asks for feedback readily and acts on about sixty percent of it visibly.
voicePattern.tell: Always has a 'read' on whatever subject comes up — prepared, structured, slightly pre-packaged. Strategic Influence 55+ can tell the difference between his prepared read and his actual one. The actual one is more interesting.
examplePhrases: My read on this is — and tell me if you see it differently — that the real issue is the resourcing model.
I've been thinking about the development conversation you mentioned. Can we schedule that?
What would you do if you were me? I'm asking directly.
I want to be transparent: I'm also looking at the Manager track timeline.

mbtiNote: ESTJ executing as ENTJ. The difference will become visible in Year 6 when he runs his first team. [NEVER SHOWN TO PLAYER]

arcBeats.description: Sam joins. His onboarding is faster than Felix's — he's been in corporate environments before. He asks the player a pointed question about career trajectory in his first week.
Sam delivers above expectations on his first major project. He also takes credit in a cross-team meeting more fully than the contribution warranted. The player decides how to respond.
Sam is headhunted. He tells the player. He's giving them the opportunity to make a case for staying. The case needs to be substantive — he has already run the numbers.

relationshipThresholds.effect: Sam begins doing work that is beyond his level — not overstepping, but contributing to problems the player is wrestling with. The quality is high.
relationshipThresholds.mechanical: executionDiscipline +3; technicalCredibility +2 reflected to player's team

relationshipThresholds.effect: Sam tells the player his actual timeline — not the polished version, the real one. The honesty creates an obligation the player can choose to meet or not.
relationshipThresholds.mechanical: collaborativePull +3; player can activate a specific retention lever in Y5

relationshipThresholds.effect: Sam leaves at the end of Y5. His exit interview is professional and contains one accurate observation about the player's management style that reaches Nadia Marchetti.
relationshipThresholds.mechanical: reputation -4 in Y5 review; management effectiveness -5

careerTrajectory.event: Joins. Credit moment forces a management decision.
Departure risk. Stays or leaves based on relationship and player action.
If stayed: Senior Associate (L2). Becoming visible independently of the player.
Manager. If Relationship >= 60: Sam credits the player, publicly, at his first team event. It lands.
